# scroll-zoom-thing — Full LLM Context

> CSS 3D perspective parallax for MkDocs Material — no JS, pure CSS

## Project overview

scroll-zoom-thing is a minimal, well-documented reference implementation of a pure-CSS 3D perspective parallax effect, extracted from the [squidfunk/mkdocs-material](https://github.com/squidfunk/mkdocs-material) hero section. It demonstrates how to use CSS `perspective`, `translateZ`, and `scale()` to create parallax scrolling without any JavaScript or scroll event listeners.

## How the parallax works

1. The scroll container gets `perspective: Npx` — this establishes a 3D rendering context
2. Each layer is a `<picture>` element with `transform: translateZ(depth)` — pushing it back in 3D space
3. Elements pushed further back appear to scroll slower (natural 3D projection — the same way distant mountains move slower than foreground trees)
4. `scale()` is applied to compensate for the apparent size reduction caused by depth, so all layers fill the viewport regardless of depth
5. No JavaScript, no scroll events — the browser handles all 3D geometry during normal scroll rendering

## Repository structure

```
docs/
├── overrides/
│   └── home.html                    # Jinja2 template — layer <picture> elements
├── assets/
│   ├── stylesheets/
│   │   └── home.css                 # All parallax CSS, heavily commented
│   └── hero/                        # Drop AVIF layer images here
│       └── .gitkeep
├── index.md
├── how-it-works.md
├── your-own-layers.md
├── advanced-css.md
├── ai-image-generation.md
├── agents.md
├── github-pages.md
└── mkdocs-skills.md
mkdocs.yml
skills/
├── SKILL.md
├── setup-parallax/SKILL.md
├── generate-prompts/SKILL.md
├── convert-images/SKILL.md
└── tune-layers/SKILL.md
.claude/
└── commands/
    ├── setup-parallax.md
    ├── generate-prompts.md
    ├── convert-images.md
    ├── tune-layers.md
    └── deploy.md
agents/
├── agent.md
├── image-agent.md
└── parallax-agent.md
```

## CSS variables

Per-layer variables set inline on each `<picture>` element:

| Variable | Type | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `--md-parallax-depth` | number | Depth — higher = slower scroll. Suggested: 8, 5, 2, 1 |
| `--md-image-position` | percentage | `object-position` horizontal % — controls visible crop |

## Agent skills

| Skill | Description |
|---|---|
| setup-parallax | Scaffolds the layer file structure and template |
| generate-prompts | Creates AI image generation prompts for each layer |
| convert-images | Converts source images to AVIF at @4x resolution |
| tune-layers | Adjusts depth and crop per layer for visual balance |

## Constraints

- No JavaScript in the parallax implementation — CSS only
- MkDocs Material theme required
- Images must be AVIF format, `@4x` suffix, named `N-description@4x.avif`
- No telemetry, no external calls, no analytics

## Credits

Parallax technique and original CSS from [squidfunk/mkdocs-material](https://github.com/squidfunk/mkdocs-material) — MIT License, copyright Martin Donath.
